content: If we adapt the language-learning lexical mastery logic of human reflexion (as a repeated, contextualized practice of grounding abstract meanings in specific use cases) to upgrade the LLM Self-Refine pipeline, we can turn vague iterative self-feedback into structured, memory-grounded refinement that aligns outputs more closely with end-user domain expectations. This framework reframes self-refinement not as just repeated correction, but as repeated contextual reflection that builds specialized lexical and structural fluency for target tasks, similar to how human learners build mastery.
